Q1 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements regarding the ‘Marginal Standing Facility (MSF)’ is correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
‘Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) is a window for scheduled banks to borrow overnight funds
from RBI in emergency situations when inter-bank liquidity dries up, using SLR securities.

Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the ‘Viksit Bharat - Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Bill, 2025’:
1. It aims to guarantee employment and livelihood in rural and urban areas.
2. It raises the minimum guaranteed wage employment days for rural households from 100 to 125 days per year.
3. It aims to replace the existing MGNREGA.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The VB - GRAM G Bill was passed to guarantee rural employment (Rozgar) and livelihood
(Ajeevika). It builds upon frameworks like MGNREGA but serves as a new mission-mode guarantee.
• It raises the minimum guaranteed wage employment days for rural households from 100 to 125 days per year.

1. India’s ranking improved to 130th with an HDI value of 0.685.
2. India is now classified under the ‘High Human Development’ category.
3. Iceland topped the index, followed by Norway.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
India remains in the ‘Medium Human Development’ category. India improved its rank to 130, and Iceland topped the list.

Correct answer: B
Solution

formal schooling, focusing on functional literacy.

Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A Goldilocks Economy is typically characterised by:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
A Goldilocks Economy is typically characterized by moderate economic growth and low inflation,
creating a stable environment for policy-making and economic activity. It is neither too hot to
cause high inflation nor too cold to cause recession or economic stagnation.

Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the Index of Eight Core Industries (ICI):
1. It is published by the Office of the Economic Adviser under the Ministry of Commerce & Industry.
2. It serves as a leading indicator for the Index of Industrial Production (IIP).
3. It is released on a quarterly basis.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The Office of the Economic Adviser, Department of Industrial Policy and Promotion, Ministry of
Commerce and Industry, compiles and publishes the Index of Eight Core Industries (ICI).
The Eight Core Industries together constitute about 40.27% of the weight in the IIP, making ICI
an important leading indicator of overall industrial performance.
The Index of Eight Core Industries is released on a monthly basis, usually at the end of the
subsequent month after the reference month.

Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the “Gini Index” recently mentioned in the World Bank reports for India:
1. It is a statistical measure of distribution intended to represent the income or wealth inequality within a nation.
2. A Gini Index of ‘0’ represents perfect inequality, while ‘100’ represents perfect equality.
3. According to the 2025 report, India’s Gini Index of 25.5 indicates it is among the world’s most equal societies.
Which of the statements above are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
In the Gini Index, 0 represents perfect equality and 100 represents perfect inequality. India’s low
score of 25.5 places it as the 4th most equal country globally behind Slovak Republic, Slovenia, and
Belarus.

Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following best defines a cess?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• A cess is a special-purpose levy imposed over and above the existing taxes (such as income
tax, GST, or excise duty).
• It is collected to raise funds for a particular objective or sector, such as education, health,
environment, or infrastructure.
• Unlike general taxes, which go into the Consolidated Fund of India or State, the proceeds
from a cess are usually earmarked for a designated fund to be used only for that purpose.

3. Intellectual Property (IP) is a legal concept which refers to creations of the mind for which exclusive rights are recognized.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

Moonlighting is a situation when person is working as employee for two different organizations in the spare time.

Not attempted

Which of the following branch of ethics deals with logical and semantic questions like ‘what do we mean by freedom and determinism’?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Meta ethics is a branch of analytic philosophy that explores the status, foundations, and scope of
moral values, properties, and words. Whereas the fields of applied ethics and normative theory
focus on what is moral, metaethics focuses on what morality itself is.

Correct answer: C

from ‘acts of humans’ which result without the intervention of intellect and free will and as such
normally they are beyond human control.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Aggregation refers to the increase in population of the species which has become established in the area.
• Ecesis is the initial establishment of a plant community. It is also called colonization.

Q21 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding Invasive Alien Species:
1. Invasive alien species are animals, plants or other organisms that are introduced by humans, either intentionally or accidentally, into places outside of their natural range.
2. The Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework aims to completely reduce the rate of invasive alien species introduction by 2040.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Invasive alien species are animals, plants or other organisms that are introduced by humans,
either intentionally or accidentally, into places outside of their natural range, negatively
impacting native biodiversity, ecosystem services or human economy and well-being.
• The Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, which aims to halt and reverse
biodiversity loss by 2030, was adopted in December 2022 at the 15th meeting of the Conference
of the Parties to the Convention on Biological Diversity.

Q27 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Frequency Division Multiple Access (FDMA) can accommodate more users in the same spectrum space than a Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA) system providing more efficient spectrum usage.
2. Code Division Multiple Access (CDMA) assigns a unique code sequence to each user that is
used to code data before transmission.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA) allows several users to share the same frequency channel
by dividing the signal into different time slots and thus, can accommodate more users in the same
spectrum space than an FDMA system providing more efficient spectrum usage. Thus, statement
1 is not correct.

Q28 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The malware which silently evolves and does long-term damage is termed as:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) is a prolonged and targeted cyber attack where hackers gain
unauthorized access to a network and remain undetected for an extended period.

Q29 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about Read Only Memory (ROM):
1. It is a non-volatile memory.
2. It is dynamic as its storage cells must be refreshed.
3. The information from ROM can only be read and it’s not possible to write fresh information on it.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
ROM is permanently built into the computer at the time of production. The data stored in ROM is
pre-programmed during the manufacturing process and cannot be modified or erased by the user
under normal conditions. It is a non-volatile memory and retains its stored data even after the
power supply is turned off. Its static nature means it doesn’t require refreshing.

Q30 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding D2M (Direct-to-Mobile) technology:
1. The technology is based on the convergence of broadband and broadcast, using which mobile phones can receive terrestrial digital TV.
2. It would be similar to how people listen to FM radio on their phones, where a receiver within the phone can tap into radio frequencies.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The Direct-to-Mobile (D2M) technology is indeed based on the convergence of broadband
and broadcast, which allows mobile phones to receive terrestrial digital TV signals without
an active internet connection.
• The scientific principle behind D2M is similar to that of an FM radio, where a dedicated
receiver chip within the device can tap into specific radio frequencies to access content, rather
than relying on cellular data networks.

Q31 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are the malware that can take over electronic devices and use them for
Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Saposhi: A malware which is capable of taking over electronic devices and turning them into
'bots' which can be used for Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack.
Gravity RAT: A Remote Access Trojan (RAT) primarily used for cyber-espionage and data theft;
targeting Windows, Android, and macOS to steal sensitive files, call logs, and contacts.
Pegasus: A spyware designed to remotely monitor smartphones by accessing cameras,
microphones, and encrypted messages.

Solution

(a)
NeVA is transforming all State Legislatures into digital houses so as to enable them to transact entire Government Business on digital platform. NeVA ensures electronic information exchange between stakeholder departments, thereby creating a seamless Government-to-Government (G2G) e-governance interaction.

Q38 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

According to Kano’s categorisation of customer requirements, which of the following will not make customer unhappy if missing in product?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Expecters: Basic attributes that one would expect to seen in the product.
Spoken: Specific features that customers say they want in product.
Unspoken: Product attributes that the customers does not generally talk about, but they
remain important to him or her.
Exciters: Product features that make it unique and distinguish from the competition.
Absence of these features will not make customer unhappy, since they do not know that is
missing.

Q53 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is NOT one of the 7-S of Project Management?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The 7-S of Project Management are:
• Strategy - High- level project objectives and approach.
• Structure - Organizational arrangement for the project.
• System - Methods for planning, monitoring, and control.
• Staff - Selection, recruitment, and leadership.
• Skill - Technical and managerial competencies.
• Style - Working style and organizational culture.
• Shared Values - Common beliefs and interests of stakeholders.
Schedule is a project planning tool, not part of the 7- S framework.

Q54 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which type of Project Management Office (PMO) has a high degree of control over projects and directly manages project resources?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Supportive PMO → Provides templates, best practices; low control.
Controlling PMO → Enforces compliance, standards; moderate control.
Directive PMO → Directly manages projects and resources; high control.

Which of the following is the correct sequence of steps in financial feasibility analysis of a project?

Solution

2. Estimation of project cash flows
3. Estimation of expected rate of return
4. Application of decision rule (NPV, IRR, B/C ratio, Payback, etc.)

Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
1. Chrome Nickel steel will have (after heat treatment) almost the same strength and ductility
as 3.5% nickel steel which has also been heat treated but it will not cost as much.
2. Chrome nickel steels combine the effect of nickel (in increasing the toughness and ductility)
and chromium (in improving hardenability and wear resistance)

Solution

Q64 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The steel containing 16 to 18% chromium and about 0.12% carbon is called

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
A steel containing 16 to 18% chromium and about 0.12% carbon is called Ferritic stainless steel.
Martensitic stainless steels contain 12–18% Cr and a high carbon content; Austenitic stainless
steels contain 18% Cr, 8% Ni, and C between 0.03-0.15% and Nickel steel contains 3.5–5% Nickel.

Q66 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The transducers used in microphones are made of

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Microphones convert sound energy into electrical signals. Piezoelectric materials generate an
electric voltage when mechanical stress is applied to them. Therefore, piezoelectric materials are
commonly used as transducers in microphones to convert acoustic signals (sound waves) into
electrical signals (voltage).

Q67 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The width of the energy gap of a superconductor is maximum at

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
In superconductors, the energy gap decreases with an increase in temperature.
According to BCS theory, at absolute zero (0 K), the energy gap is maximum. As the temperature
approaches the transition temperature, the energy gap reduces to zero. At transition temperature,
the material loses its superconducting properties and becomes a normal conductor.

Q68 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Resistivity of electrical conductors is most affected by

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Temperature strongly affects the energy and distribution of charge carriers. An increase in
temperature provides thermal energy to electrons, enabling them to move to higher energy levels,
thereby affecting resistivity.

Q89 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In a certain code language, ‘PEA’ means ‘TCY’, ‘EAR’ means ‘CTP’ and ‘ATE’ means ‘RTC’ then find the code of P?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Comparing PEA, EAR and ATE, we see that EA is common in all the three words. Thus, the the code for EA will be T and C in some order as it is also common in all the three codewords. Thus, the code for P will be Y.

(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true but Statement (II) is not a correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Q92 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The following items consists of two statements, one labelled as Statement (I) and the other labelled as Statement (II). You have to examine these two statements carefully and select your answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true but Statement (II) is not a correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): A chip card’s operating system (COS) is a sequence of instructions permanently embedded in the ROM of the smart card.
Statement (II): COS instructions provide functions for data and command exchange, data storage, data processing, cryptographic processes and more.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Every smart card has an operating system (OS).
• A chip card’s operating system (COS) is a sequence of instructions permanently embedded in the ROM of the smart card.
• COS instructions are frequently used by on-card processes and applications. They provide
functions for data and command exchange, data storage, data processing, cryptographic processes and more.

Q94 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The following items consists of two statements, one labelled as Statement (I) and the other labelled as Statement (II). You have to examine these two statements carefully and select your answers to these items using the codes given below:

(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true but Statement (II) is not a correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Project execution plan is developed to manage complex projects.
Statement (II): In a complex project, there may exist thousands of variables, both tangible and intangible.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Project Execution Plan (PEP) is essential to systematically manage and control complex projects.
Reason Complex projects involve numerous tangible and intangible variables (resources, risks,
stakeholders, uncertainties). The presence of many interacting variables necessitates a structured
execution plan.

Q95 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The following items consists of two statements, one labelled as Statement (I) and the other labelled as Statement (II). You have to examine these two statements carefully and select your answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are true but Statement (II) is not a correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Ways and Means Advances (WMA) is a temporary advance given by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to both the Central and State governments to meet temporary mismatches in their receipts and payments.
Statement (II): The interest rate on WMA is linked to the RBI’s Reverse Repo Rate.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Ways and Means Advances (WMA) is a temporary advance given by the Reserve Bank of
India (RBI) to both the Central and State governments to meet temporary mismatches in their
receipts and payments. This facility helps the government manage short-term liquidity issues
and is repayable generally within three months.
• WMA cannot be used as a permanent instrument for financing the fiscal deficit. It is meant
only for temporary mismatches in cash flows and is not a long-term borrowing mechanism.
• The interest rate on WMA is linked to the RBI’s Repo Rate.
